items.name: Automatic Data Processing, Inc.
items.headline: ADP enters new $3.5B five-year credit agreement
whatChanged: New $3.5B five-year revolving credit agreement dated June 26, 2026, replacing prior 364-Day and Five-Year Credit Agreements
Facility split into US Tranche ($2.94B), Canadian Tranche ($325.7M), and Euro Tranche ($238M) with June 26, 2031 maturity
Proceeds earmarked for general corporate purposes including refinancing of existing credit agreements
Pricing grid tied to credit ratings with commitment fees ranging 4.0–10.0 bps and term benchmark spreads 62.5–112.5 bps
items.decisionTakeaway: The new $3.5B revolver refinances existing facilities with no change to overall liquidity strategy, maintaining ADP's strong investment-grade access to capital.

items.name: Ameren Corporation
items.headline: Ameren Missouri seeks $343M electric rate increase from MoPSC
whatChanged: Ameren Missouri filed for a $343M annual electric revenue increase, based on 10.25% ROE and 52% equity capital structure
Rate base of $16.7B with test year ended March 31, 2026 and pro-forma adjustments through December 31, 2026
Request includes 400 MW of new generation investments and continued use of fuel adjustment clause and trackers
MoPSC decision expected by May 2027 with new rates effective June 2027
items.decisionTakeaway: The $343M rate case filing signals continued capital investment growth at Ameren Missouri with regulatory recovery expected mid-2027, supporting the utility's earnings trajectory.

items.name: The AES Corporation
items.headline: AES stockholders approve $15.00/share GIP/EQT buyout
whatChanged: Stockholders voted ~97.92% in favor of the acquisition by Global Infrastructure Partners (BlackRock) and EQT-led Consortium, representing ~67.17% of outstanding shares
Merger terms: $15.00/share cash, ~$10.7B equity value, ~$33.4B enterprise value (proportional net debt $22.7B as of Dec 31, 2025)
Transaction expected to close late 2026 or early 2027, remaining subject to federal, state, and foreign regulatory approvals
Prior comparable filing (June 16) was a $1.0B senior notes underwriting agreement ($600M 5.200% due 2029; $400M 5.750% due 2033) — no overlap in subject matter
items.decisionTakeaway: With stockholder approval secured, the deal now hinges solely on regulatory clearances; the $15.00 cash price caps upside and the spread to close will be driven by deal-timeline risk.

keyMetrics.voteInFavor: 97.92% of votes cast (67.17% of outstanding shares)

items.name: Carnival Corporation Ltd.
items.headline: DLC unification completed, buybacks begin, data breach litigation
whatChanged: DLC unification completed in May 2026: Carnival plc shares delisted, single Carnival Corporation Ltd. entity listed on NYSE; redomiciled from Panama to Bermuda; $2.2B reduction in additional paid-in capital and $2.6B reduction in treasury stock from share exchange
Share repurchase program commenced: $381M repurchased in H1 FY2026 under the $2.5B authorization approved in March 2026; first buybacks since pre-pandemic
New data security incident disclosed: six consolidated class actions filed in S.D. Florida related to April 14, 2026 cyber incident; consolidated in May 2026; company believes outcome not material

Havana Docks litigation update: Supreme Court vacated 11th Circuit ruling on May 21, 2026 and remanded for further proceedings; Italian COVID-19 appellate court reversed lower court, ordering non-material damages
Debt reduced by $1.8B from Nov 30, 2025 to May 31, 2026 ($27.4B to $25.6B); convertible notes settled in Dec 2025; interest expense declined to $285M in Q2 from $341M prior year
items.decisionTakeaway: DLC unification simplifies corporate structure while $2.5B buyback authorization signals management confidence in cash generation, though new data breach litigation and rising fuel costs warrant monitoring.

items.name: Expand Energy Corporation
items.headline: VP Accounting & Controller Larson resigns; CFO Teunissen assumes interim PAO role
whatChanged: Gregory M. Larson, VP – Accounting & Controller, notified the Company on June 23, 2026 of his intention to resign; no disagreement with the Company cited
CFO Marcel Teunissen (appointed EVP/CFO April 6, 2026) has assumed principal accounting officer duties on an interim basis while a permanent replacement is sought
No new compensatory arrangements, transactions, or family relationships disclosed for Teunissen beyond his existing CFO indemnification agreement
items.decisionTakeaway: Routine VP-level accounting departure with experienced CFO covering interim and no disagreement disclosed — unlikely to alter the investment thesis but warrants monitoring for succession timing.
